title: Else If
description: Handle multiple conditions in a row.

slides.title: More Than Two Choices 🛤️
slides.content:
## Creating a Chain

Sometimes a simple True/False split isn't enough. 

What if we want to assign a letter grade (A, B, C) based on a score? We need out program to check multiple conditions in order.

slides.title: Chaining with 'else if' 🔗
slides.content:
We can add an `else if` block immediately after an `if` block. 

The program checks the first condition. If it's false, it moves to the second one. If that's false, it moves to the `else`.

quizData.question: If we set `score = 95` in the previous code, what gets printed?
quizData.explanation: Because 95 >= 90 is true, it prints 'Grade: A'. Once a true block is found, it ignores the rest of the chain.

slides.title: Order Matters! ⚠️
slides.content:
## Top to Bottom

The computer evaluates the `if / else if` chain purely from **top to bottom**.

As soon as it finds *one* condition that is `true`, it runs that block and **skips everything else below it**.

You must put your most specific or highest conditions at the top!

quizData.question: What is wrong with this logic? `if (score >= 50) { print("Pass") } else if (score >= 90) { print("A") }`

options.text: It will never print 'A' because 90 is also >= 50, triggering the first block.

quizData.explanation: If the score is 95, the top condition `score >= 50` is true, so it prints 'Pass' and stops checking.
